evidence_quotes.quote: any developer who wishes to distribute their app will need to use Google Play for that.

paragraphs.text: Google wants to restrict the process of installing applications on Android devices exclusively to their own proprietary solution. It means that any developer who wishes to distribute their app will need to use Google Play for that. They plan to roll out this feature somewhere in the year 2026. The users will be forced to use the apps exclusively approved by Google. Since they have proven time and time again that they cannot be trusted with following the user privacy regulations, giving them that much power seems like a terrible idea. It also directly combats the recent movement of DeGoogle, which promotes seeking more privacy-focused alternatives to Google services. It does that by killing all competition on the app store front. As an example, the F-DROID app store will be unusable, since all of their apps are served outside Google Play. It would very much be a monopoly. Taking a firm stance against their initiative would very much be in line with the Digital Fairness Act.
